steps.text: The line of best fit in a scatterplot summarizes the overall trend. Identify whether it slopes upward (positive association) or downward (negative association) as you move left to right along the x-axis (elevation).

The line runs from the upper-left (low elevation, higher temperature) to the lower-right (high elevation, lower temperature), indicating a negative slope. As elevation increases, temperature decreases.

Evaluate every choice:
  • A: "As elevation increases, temperature tends to increase." — Describes a positive slope. The line slopes downward, so this is wrong. Eliminated.
  • B: "As elevation increases, temperature tends to decrease." — Matches the negative slope of the line of best fit. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: "As elevation decreases, temperature tends to decrease." — If elevation decreases (move left), the line shows temperature increases, not decreases. Eliminated.
  • D: "There is no association." — The line of best fit clearly trends downward, so an association exists. Eliminated.
